inputContract.authorIntent: explain why AI tool upgrades do not automatically improve operator capability
inputContract.targetReader: content and product operators using AI tools
inputContract.toneBoundary: direct, practical, no fake personal story

findings: The original draft used broad benefits such as efficiency and lower creation barrier without concrete operator actions.
The original claim said operators should learn new tools but did not separate useful adoption from shallow tool-chasing.
The same generic paragraph could not work well across Xiaohongshu, X, and Zhihu.

rewritesApplied: Replaced broad AI-upgrade framing with operator-specific decisions.
Created separate Xiaohongshu, X, and Zhihu versions with different rhythm and structure.
Kept X output in Chinese because the user did not request English.
preservedClaims: AI tools can improve production efficiency.
Operators still need to learn and adapt.
Competitive advantage comes from how people use the tools, not the tool list itself.

notes: X version is compact and opinion-led.
Xiaohongshu version is saveable and checklist-based.
Zhihu version explains the boundary and reasoning.

finalDecision.reason: The rewrite preserves the user's claim, adds platform-native structure, avoids invented facts, and passes the final anti-AI check.
